json.title: ETIC, Summer Internship - Data Analytics
json.descriptionHtml:
Line of Service
Advisory

Industry/Sector
Technology

Specialism
Advisory - Other

Management Level
Intern/Trainee

Job Description & Summary



We are opening applications for our Summer Internship Program 2026, designed for students graduating in 2027. This program provides hands-on experience in the Data Analytics practice, with interns assigned to one of two specialized tracks: Business Intelligence (BI) or Data Engineering.

Interns will work on real-world projects, learning how to transform data into insights, build scalable solutions, and support decision-making across diverse business contexts.
Key Responsibilities
Depending on assigned track, interns will:

  • Business Intelligence:

    • Assist in building dashboards and reports using BI tools.
    • Support data visualization and storytelling for business stakeholders.
    • Contribute to performance tracking and KPI analysis.
  • Data Engineering:

    • Help design and maintain data pipelines.
    • Support ETL processes and data integration.
    • Work with cloud-based data platforms to ensure scalability and reliability.

Across both tracks:

  • Collaborate with mentors and peers to deliver project outcomes.
  • Participate in Agile ceremonies, knowledge-sharing sessions, and team stand-ups.
  • Document learnings and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives.
Qualifications
  • Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s program (Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Business Informatics, or related fields).
  • Expected graduation: 2027.
  • Strong academic foundation in programming, databases, or analytics concepts.
  • Familiarity with at least one of the following areas:

    • BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik)
    • Data engineering tools (SQL, Python, Spark, Databricks)
  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.
  • Eagerness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment.
.Program Details

  • Location: Cairo

json.title: Consulting Intern - COOP Program
json.descriptionHtml:
About Us

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

Join Visa and do work that matters – to you, to your community, and to the world. Progress starts with you.

Job Description

Visa Consulting & Analytics (VCA) is the payments consulting arm of Visa, focused on serving Visa’s clients and partners. We are a global team of more than 600 consultants and data scientists. We offer consulting and analytics services across different areas of interest to our clients including strategy, product, portfolio management, risk, digital and more.

Within VCA, we have a dedicated Data Science team who are experts in statistics, advanced analytics and machine learning with exclusive access to insights from VisaNet, one of the largest payment networks in the world. The majority of our team members are generalists and get exposure to all the topics we offer and all the countries we cover.

During Your VCA Internship Assignment, You Will Get To Work Closely With a Consulting Project Team To Support In Different Aspects Of The Project Delivery, Including For Example

  • Collecting required data and conducting research
  • Conducting data analysis using internal and external data sources
  • Developing insights from the data analyses to support the recommendations development process
  • Developing project deliverables including slides, excel models, etc.
  • Supporting the project team members in developing hypotheses, contribute to conclusions and assisting other senior team members in presenting back to the client
  • Supporting the team in presenting the recommendations to the client

In addition to contributing to consulting projects, you may also get the chance to support VCA team members in developing white papers or internal research, as well as client proposals.

VCA projects (as well as proposals) can be with clients across the region and can be across the different VCA focus areas (portfolio, digital, risk, etc.) depending on the project pipeline.

Qualifications

  • Current student working towards a Bachelors or Masters degree, with a graduation date of December 2026 or summer of 2027
  • Economics, Business, Finance, Economics, Econometrics IT/MIS, Engineering or related disciplines welcome to apply
  • Available for the internship (June – August 2026)
  • Must be available to start a graduate role by June 2027
  • Excellent academic record
  • Strong analytical capabilities and experience solving business cases
  • Strong in MS Excel, Word & PowerPoint
  • Superior analytical and financial analysis skills
  • Strong collaboration skills with a diverse peer group required
  • Able to articulate viewpoint effectively to key stakeholders in order to get buy in and agreement

Visa is an EEO Employer

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

json.title: Sports Data Collector (Football) - Dayrout, Egypt
json.descriptionHtml:
Sports Data Collector (Sports Scout)

Independent Contractor | Paid per game

  • Please note: All CVs are required to be submitted in English.

Love sports? Turn your passion into paid experience.

Genius Sports is a global sports technology company powering official live data for the world's biggest leagues, federations, and media platforms.

We're recruiting Sports Data Collector (Sports Scout) to attend live sporting events and collect real-time, play-by-play data using our proprietary software.

What you'll do

  • Attend live sports events in your local area from Egypt Premier League and lower profile competitions
  • Record accurate, real-time game data (scores, plays, key events)
  • Use mobile technology in a fast-paced, live environment

What we're looking for

  • Strong knowledge of the sport
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, and organized
  • Availability on evenings and weekends
  • 18+ years old

What we offer

  • Fixed payment per game - 40 €
  • Flexible, game-based work
  • Training and testing provided
  • Opportunity to increase earnings based on performance

Interested?

Tell us a bit about yourself and we'll be in touch.

👉 Learn more about our Statisticians Network:

https://www.geniussports.com/careers/statisticians-network/

Genius Sports is an equal opportunities employer.

json.companyDescription:
NYSE: GENI

Genius is the operating system for modern sport. Building technology and experiences to make sports even better.

Our solutions across the global sports ecosystem are underpinned by our next generation big data and artificial intelligence platform, GeniusIQ. 

By ingesting and computing multiple real-time data feeds, GeniusIQ powers a wide range of functions and applications across sports performance and officiation, fan engagement, advertising, and sports betting.

Founded in 2001, Genius Sports is now one of the world’s largest sports technology companies, working with the likes of the NFL, NBA, EPL, FIBA, ESPN, Sky Sports, Flutter, DraftKings and 100s more. 

We're headquartered in London and New York and have offices Los Angeles, Medellin, Tallinn, Sofia, Singapore, Lausanne, Manchester and Melbourne.

Visit our Showcase Pages to discover more about our products.

json.title: Junior Robotics Data Collector
json.descriptionHtml:
CNTXT AI is a leading artificial intelligence company focused on building next-generation AI systems, products, and infrastructure. We specialize in developing multimodal AI, robotics, and intelligent agents, powered by high-quality real-world data. Data is at the core of our innovation. We invest heavily in data collection, annotation, and AI training pipelines to enable advanced systems such as embodied AI and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.


Role Summary


CNTXT AI is looking for a Robotics Data Collector to support the collection of high-quality robotics and egocentric data used to train AI models.

This role involves working hands-on with robotic systems, sensors, and real-world environments to capture multimodal datasets, including video, motion, and interaction data. You will play a key role in enabling the development of intelligent robotic systems and AI agents.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in lab and controlled real-world environments.


Key Responsibilities


1. Data Collection Execution

  • Collect robotics and egocentric (first-person) data using:
  • Cameras (handheld, head-mounted, or robot-mounted)
  • Sensors (IMU, depth cameras, etc.)
  • Execute data collection tasks based on predefined scenarios and protocols.
  • Ensure consistent and accurate recording of actions, interactions, and environments.


2. Robotics Operation Support

  • Assist in operating robotic systems (humanoid or mobile robots) during data collection.
  • Support setup and calibration of:
  • Cameras and sensors
  • Recording systems
  • Ensure proper functioning of hardware during experiments.


3. Data Quality & Validation

  • Review collected data for:
  • Completeness
  • Clarity
  • Accuracy
  • Identify and flag issues such as:
  • Missing data
  • Recording errors
  • Sensor misalignment
  • Follow quality guidelines to ensure high usability for AI training.


4. Experiment Setup & Execution

  • Prepare environments for data collection sessions.
  • Execute tasks such as:
  • Object manipulation
  • Navigation scenarios
  • Human-robot interaction recordings
  • Follow detailed instructions to simulate real-world use cases.


5. Documentation & Reporting

  • Maintain records of:
  • Data collection sessions
  • Equipment usage
  • Issues encountered
  • Provide feedback to improve data collection processes and protocols.


6. Safety & Compliance

  • Follow all lab safety protocols and operational guidelines.
  • Handle robotics equipment and sensors with care.
  • Ensure compliance with data privacy and security requirements.


Required Qualifications


  • 0–3+ years of experience
  • Data collection, field operations, or lab environments
  • Technical or hardware-related roles
  • Comfortable working with:
  • Cameras, sensors, and recording devices
  • Basic computer systems and tools
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to follow instructions precisely.


Preferred Skills

  • Experience with robotics systems or hardware environments.
  • Familiarity with:
  • Egocentric or first-person data collection
  • Computer vision or AI-related datasets
  • Basic understanding of:
  • Robotics concepts
  • Sensors and data recording tools
  • Experience working in technical labs or research environments.


Key Traits

  • Detail-oriented and quality-focused
  • Hands-on and execution-driven
  • Reliable and consistent in following protocols
  • Comfortable working in dynamic environments
  • Curious about robotics and AI


Success Metrics

  • Volume and quality of data collected
  • Accuracy and completeness of datasets
  • Adherence to data collection protocols
  • Reduction in data errors and rework
  • Efficiency in executing data collection tasks


Why Join CNTXT AI

  • Work on cutting-edge robotics and AI systems
  • Contribute to building next-generation intelligent agents
  • Gain hands-on experience with robotics labs and real-world data
  • Be part of a fast-growing, innovation-driven company

json.companyDescription:
Data powers AI. We make sure yours is ready for it.

CNTXT AI is a data-first AI company that helps organizations prepare, build, deploy, and scale localized AI solutions without compromising data sovereignty. Our enterprise services include high-quality training data through labeling and annotation for AI labs, enterprise data teams, and robotics applications, alongside custom AI solutions that integrate with clients' existing infrastructure. Our proprietary AI product portfolio includes Munsit, the leading Arabic voice AI.

From raw data to production-ready AI, we help organizations and governments move faster without sacrificing compliance, sovereignty, or real-world performance.

json.title: Customer Data Exchange Analyst
json.descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Customer Data Exchange Analyst

Egypt

Full Time / Hybrid

40 hours

We make health happen

At Bupa, our purpose is simple – helping people live longer, healthier, happier lives and making a better world. As a Customer Data Exchange Analyst, you’ll play a key role in supporting this by helping us manage and share customer data safely, accurately and efficiently.

This role sits at the heart of how we connect systems, teams and services to create a seamless customer experience. You’ll work with data flows and integrations that enable us to deliver care and services in a way that is reliable and trusted.

What makes this role unique is the opportunity to work on meaningful data that directly impacts customer outcomes, while being part of an organisation that puts wellbeing at the centre of everything it does. You’ll collaborate across teams, contribute to improving how data is exchanged, and help ensure we meet both operational and regulatory requirements.

Key Responsibilities

As a Customer Data Exchange Analyst, you’ll:

  • Support the design, monitoring and improvement of customer data exchange processes
  • Help ensure data is transferred accurately and securely between systems
  • Investigate and resolve data-related issues, working with both technical and non-technical teams
  • Monitor data flows and identify opportunities to improve efficiency and reliability
  • Assist with data mapping, validation and transformation activities
  • Work with stakeholders to understand data needs and ensure solutions meet business requirements
  • Support compliance with data governance, privacy and security standards
  • Document processes clearly to support consistency and continuous improvement

What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who is curious, detail-focused and enjoys working with data.

You’ll Ideally Have

  • Experience working with data exchange, data integration or data analysis
  • An understanding of how data moves between systems (for example, via APIs, files or databases)
  • Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
  • The ability to communicate clearly with a range of stakeholders
  • Experience identifying and fixing data issues
  • A basic understanding of data privacy and security principles

It would also be helpful (but not essential) if you have:

  • Experience working in a regulated environment such as healthcare or financial services
  • Familiarity with tools used for data analysis or integration
  • An interest in improving processes and ways of working

Why Bupa?

Bupa Global is the international health insurance division of Bupa. We provide customers who want premium international coverage with products and services to access the healthcare they need anytime, around the world, whether at home or when studying, living, travelling or working abroad.

Bupa Global has offices around the world including London and Brighton (UK), Dublin (Europe), Miami (USA), Dubai (UAE, in partnership with OIC), Egypt and Hong Kong (China) as well as regional offices in mainland China, Singapore, the Dominican Republic, Bolivia, Panama, Guatemala and Ecuador.

Time Type

Full time

Job Area

IT

Locations:

Egypt - Cairo

We are a healthcare company serving millions of customers. With no shareholders, we reinvest profits into providing more and better healthcare for the benefit of current and future customers. 
For more information, visit www.bupa.co.uk

json.title: Senior Reporting & Advanced Analytics Analyst
json.descriptionHtml:
About this Position The position is part of the Global Business Solution in Cairo (GBS+ Cairo), in the Market Data Solutions Process specifically handling the operation of Senior Reporting & Advanced Data Analyst.

What You´ll Do

  • Execute and Lead market, client, and customer reporting, and perform data analysis across key business areas such as market share, brand performance, sales effectiveness, promotions, and pricing.
  • Perform data extraction, processing, and transformation to support reporting and analytics solutions using Excel, VBA, Power Query, KNIME, and Python, sourcing data from multiple systems while ensuring accuracy, quality, and consistency.
  • Design and build data models following best-practice methodologies (e.g., Star Schema, Snowflake Schema) with strong structuring, scalability, and best practice governance.
  • Develop, enhance, and troubleshoot sophisticated Power BI dashboards using complex DAX, advanced modeling, incremental refresh, and optimized performance for large/ complex datasets.
  • Ensure data reliability through rigorous preprocessing, validation, and quality checks.
  • Drive reporting automation, process optimization, and system enhancement initiatives, ensuring continuous improvement and innovation.
  • Mentor and support team members by sharing technical knowledge, best practices, and analytical methodologies.
  • Support the team in building, optimizing, and troubleshooting Power BI reports and dashboards, including handling large and complex datasets.

What makes you a good fit

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Informatics, Information Systems, or a related field.
  • 4–6 years of hands-on experience in business analysis, reporting, and data analytics
  • Fluent in English (written and spoken).
  • Strong expertise in Power BI, including advanced DAX, applying Row level security, large-data modeling following best-practice methodologies (e.g., Star Schema, Snowflake Schema), and performance optimization
  • Strong knowledge of databases, data concepts and SQL for data transformation activities.
  • Hands-on experience or working knowledge in Python for data transformation, data analysis, and automation
  • Experience with KNIME and/or Alteryx is a plus.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel (advanced functions, Power Query, VBA) and PowerPoint.
  • Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and the ability to translate data into meaningful business insights.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills.
  • FMCG Background is a huge plus.

Some perks of joining Henkel

  • Flexible work scheme with flexible hours, hybrid work model, and work from anywhere policy for up to 30 days per year
  • Diverse national and international growth opportunities
  • Global wellbeing standards with health and preventive care programs
  • Gender-neutral parental leave for a minimum of 8 weeks
  • Employee Share Plan with voluntary investment and Henkel matching shares
  • Comprehensive Health Insurance for employee + dependents
  • Employee Assistance Programme provides a wide range of mental health and wellbeing benefits

At Henkel, we come from a broad range of backgrounds, perspectives, and life experiences. We believe the uniqueness of all our employees is the power in us. Become part of the team and bring your uniqueness to us! We look for a diverse team of individuals who possess different backgrounds, experiences, personalities and mindsets.

json.companySlogan: Leading with our innovations, brands and technologies. We are: Pioneers at heart for the good of generations.
json.companyDescription:
Henkel operates worldwide with leading innovations, brands and technologies in two business areas: Adhesive Technologies and Consumer Brands.

Founded in 1876, Henkel looks back on more than 145 years of success. The company holds leading positions with its two business units in both industrial and consumer businesses thanks to strong brands, innovations and technologies such as Persil, Schwarzkopf and Loctite. Henkel, headquartered in Düsseldorf / Germany, counts among the most internationally aligned German-based companies in the global marketplace.

Our company purpose expresses what unites us all at Henkel: Pioneers at heart for the good of generations. We are a diverse team of about 47,000 colleagues worldwide, striving to enrich and improve life every day through our products, services, and solutions. Our Purpose is built from our roots and carries a long-standing legacy of innovation, responsibility, and sustainability into the future. Our shared values and Leadership Commitments guide our decisions and actions every day.

Imprint: http://www.henkel.com/imprint
Data Privacy: https://www.henkel.com/data-protection-statement-social-media

json.title: Reporting & Advanced Analytics
json.descriptionHtml:
About This Position

The position is part of the Global Business Solution in Cairo (GBS+Cairo), in the Market Data Solutions Process specifically handling the operation of Reporting & Advanced Data Analyst.

What You´ll Do

  • Execute market, client, and customer reporting, and perform data analysis across key business areas such as market share, brand performance, sales effectiveness, promotions, and pricing.
  • Perform data extraction, processing, and transformation to support reporting and analytics solutions using Excel, VBA, Power Query, KNIME, and Python, sourcing data from multiple systems while ensuring accuracy, quality, and consistency.
  • Design and build data models following best-practice methodologies (e.g., Star Schema, Snowflake Schema).
  • Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using Excel and Power BI, integrating existing and new data sources to deliver clear, actionable insights to stakeholders.
  • Support data preprocessing, analysis, and validation activities to ensure reliable outputs.
  • Participate in projects related to system enhancements, reporting automation, and process improvement initiatives.
  • Proactively share knowledge within the team and contribute to continuous improvement of analytics practices.
  • Support the team in building, optimizing, and troubleshooting Power BI reports and dashboards, including handling large and complex datasets.

What makes you a good fit

  • Bachelor’s degree in Management, Information System, Business Informatics, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • 2–4 years of relevant experience in data analysis, business analysis, or reporting roles.
  • Fluent in English (written and spoken).
  • Strong expertise in Power BI, including DAX and data modeling.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel (advanced functions, Power Query, VBA) and PowerPoint.
  • Solid foundation in databases and data concepts.
  • Hands-on experience or working knowledge of SQL, Python, KNIME, and/or Alteryx is preferred.
  • Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and the ability to translate data into meaningful business insights.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills.

Some perks of joining Henkel

  • Flexible work scheme with flexible hours, hybrid work model, and work from anywhere policy for up to 30 days per year
  • Diverse national and international growth opportunities
  • Global wellbeing standards with health and preventive care programs
  • Gender-neutral parental leave for a minimum of 8 weeks
  • Employee Share Plan with voluntary investment and Henkel matching shares
  • Comprehensive Health Insurance for employee + dependents
  • Employee Assistance Programme provides a wide range of mental health and wellbeing benefits

At Henkel, we come from a broad range of backgrounds, perspectives, and life experiences. We believe the uniqueness of all our employees is the power in us. Become part of the team and bring your uniqueness to us! We look for a diverse team of individuals who possess different backgrounds, experiences, personalities and mindsets.

json.title: Operations Associate - Data & Technology
json.descriptionHtml:
Remote (EST, 9 AM – 5 PM)

About Darkroom

Darkroom is the leading next-generation growth marketing firm engineering the brands of tomorrow. Founded in 2017, we set out to redefine what a modern agency could be by replacing the inertia of legacy advertising behemoths with a model built for speed, intelligence, and measurable impact.

At our core, Darkroom is a human services company powered by Shadow, a universal AI commerce layer that integrates executive-level strategy with proprietary agentic technology. This fusion enables our teams to deliver outsized returns by enhancing creative output, operational efficiency, and revenue generation across every stage of the customer journey.

Our track record speaks for itself: billions in attributable revenue driven across e-commerce marketplaces, media networks, DTC ecosystems, and social commerce platforms. Every engagement feeds into our proprietary data infrastructure, enabling a continuous feedback loop that accelerates growth, improves margins, and compounds results across our client portfolio.

What began as a boutique design studio has evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000) and among the most effective performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos). Our founders were recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 for advancing the intersection of technology, marketing, and advertising, cementing Darkroom's role as a defining player in the future of media innovation.

About The Role

We are hiring an Operations Associate to work across three areas: managing our proprietary Flight Plan infrastructure for e-commerce clients, designing and maintaining internal automations across our stack, and helping evaluate and deploy AI and emerging technology.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who thrives at the intersection of operational rigor, technical fluency, and client partnership. You will report to the VP of Finance and partner closely with operations, client service teams, and leadership.

This is a fully remote role supporting a team in the EST time zone (9 AM–5 PM EST). Hub locations include Brazil, Portugal and Spain.

What You'll DoFlight Plan Infrastructure

The Flight Plan is Darkroom's proprietary financial and operational infrastructure built specifically for e-commerce brands. It includes dashboard systems and P&L frameworks designed to give clients real-time visibility into their business performance. You will:

  • Own the full lifecycle of the Flight Plan from client onboarding through full operational deployment.
  • Ensure each client's Flight Plan is properly set up, maintained, and generating actionable insights.
  • Monitor the health and accuracy of each client's operational and financial dashboards — including P&Ls, revenue tracking, and margin reporting.
  • Proactively identify gaps or inefficiencies in a client's infrastructure and drive resolution.
  • Ensure clients are actively using and benefiting from the insights derived from their Flight Plan.

Automations & Integrations

You design and deploy workflows across our internal stack (Zapier, Slack, Google Workspace, Fireflies, Notion, and more). You will:

  • Build and maintain automations that connect platforms and remove manual work.
  • Audit existing automations, catch weak points early, and keep everything running reliably.
  • Document workflows and integrations so the team can rely on them without you in the room.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to surface pain points and design fit-for-purpose solutions.

AI & Emerging Technology

You stay current on AI tools and operational technology trends. You will:

  • Experiment with LLMs and emerging tools and translate capability into operational value.
  • Help build AI-enhanced workflows that make the team faster and sharper.
  • Support leadership in evaluating new platforms and surface clear, well-scoped recommendations.
  • Document systems and adoption guidelines to support rollout across the team.

What We're Looking For

Must-Have

  • Proficient in SQL (BigQuery preferred) — comfortable with joins, nested schemas such as Shopify and Amazon Seller Central data, and aggregation logic.
  • Comfortable working with Python for automation and APIs, including data extraction, Cloud Functions, scheduled jobs, and ETL workflows.
  • Hands-on experience designing and deploying no-code or low-code automations (Zapier, Make, n8n, or similar) and working knowledge of API integrations across SaaS platforms.
  • Working knowledge of data warehousing and cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), including BigQuery architecture and pipeline automation.
  • Fluent in English and comfortable in client-facing roles, including direct conversations with US-based e-commerce founders and operators.
  • Proficient in Excel and Google Sheets.
  • Organized, detail-oriented, and relentless about accuracy.
  • Preference for ownership over supervision and execution over theory.
  • Able to manage a complete financial and operational infrastructure — including dashboards and P&Ls — and ensure systems are clean, accurate, and actionable.

Nice-to-Have

  • Experience specifically with Shopify and Amazon Seller Central data schemas.
  • Familiarity with Darkroom's internal stack: Slack, Notion, Fireflies, Google Workspace.
  • Experience applying LLMs and AI tools to operational workflows to improve speed and output quality.
  • Prior experience at an agency or with DTC / e-commerce brands.
  • Background working with P&Ls and financial dashboards in a client-facing context.

What It's Like To Work At Darkroom

Darkroom is not a typical agency. We are looking for A players who want to build something great. We move fast, think deeply, and expect every team member to bring insight and rigor to everything they touch. We hold a high bar for performance, creativity, and ownership, but we also support each other relentlessly. No egos, no red tape — just world-class talent building something remarkable.

We believe in autonomy with accountability, truth over comfort, and outcomes over optics.

Unlimited PTO + Local Holidays Rebooting is part of the work. Take the time you need to stay sharp.

Remote-First Culture Many roles are fully remote. Employees based in or near our New York or Lisbon HQs are expected to work hybrid with weekly in-office time. Hub locations include Brazil and Spain.

Parental Leave Flexible parental leave to support new parents during this important transition.

Growth Our interdisciplinary model gives every Darkroomer exposure far beyond their core role. Grow your skills, expand your influence, and stay at the forefront of the industry.

Darkroom is an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, or veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in accordance with applicable laws.

json.companySlogan: Unlock the next stage of growth.
json.companyDescription:
Darkroom is a technology-driven growth marketing firm focused on growth-stage consumer companies. We were founded in 2017 out of a conviction that the iconic ad agencies of tomorrow would look very different. 

Darkroom is a human services firm built on a universal AI commerce layer called Shadow. Our model deploys a unique blend of senior, human resources enriched by an agentic technology stack that enhances output, efficiency, and revenue generation for our clients. 

Our team has been responsible for billions of dollars in trackable revenue across the various e-commerce marketplace, direct-to-consumer, and social commerce programs under management. Using research aggregated across these engagements, we accelerate results for our portfolio by developing high-impact digital strategies that unlock gateways to revenue growth, transform marketing operations, and prioritize profitability.

What started as a boutique design agency evolved into one of the fastest-growing private companies in America (Inc. 5000), one of the most successful results-driven performance media agencies of the 2020s (Varos), and esteemed recognition by Forbes 30 Under 30 for our founders’ contribution in Marketing & Advertising (Forbes).

SERVICES:
Darkroom strictly focuses on developing the next-generation of digital services. We choose to add impact in the areas where brands presently need them most and over-index on e-commerce marketplace, direct-to-consumer, and social commerce services that deliver trackable revenue. Core Darkroom service verticals include E-commerce Marketplace Management, Retail Media, Paid Media Management, Performance Creative, Social Commerce, and Customer Retention, among others. We are the only performance agency with a design-grade creative team and a certified Chief Creative Officer. These creative sensibilities are infused into our growth marketing.

Darkroom is sister to Shadow, our commerce technology layer.

json.title: Data Operation & Support Specialist/ Analyst/ Senior Analyst
json.descriptionHtml:
Role Description

Provides on time technical data application support, Problem solving & monitoring during all operational hours to insure healthy environment of supported systems and Provide smooth business operations & business continuity.

Requirements

Core Responsibilities:

  • Implement end to end to apply the data strategy scope for operation and support the enterprise data management functions
  • Effectively use supporting tools, data accessibility, logs and narrative to identify and present / data issues root cause for different data functions
  • Implement and manage the process of production data operation , deployments , documentation and population to ensure adherence to corporate data operation management
  • Work closely with all data functions manage and apply all support processes undertaken in compliance with bank policy, internal controls, data protection legislation and relevant national / industry standards
  • Identify opportunities through which to deliver process efficiency / improvement and develop data automation proposals through which to communicate and deliver the change
  • Apply the process of data backup / restore and data archiving solutions
  • Ensure the availability of data throughout the data lifecycle
  • Monitor the performance of data solutions , layers and transactions
  • Connect database standards from DBA to data support Requirements across data management Layers
  • Provide technical support to new data product and participate in new projects

Core Competencies:

  • Performance Excellence
  • Collab. & Creating Synergy
  • Agility & Resilience
  • People Centricity

Technical Competencies:

  • Sourcing, accessing and supporting data and engineering data processes
  • Excellent technical and support skills, including the use of both administration tools and technology for troubleshooting
  • Data operation model expert
  • Experience in DWH
  • Very good knowledge in Unix & Linux operating systems
  • Very good knowledge in oracle SQL & PL-SQL

Domain Expertise:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science/engineering is preferred
  • Master/ Certificate preferred
  • Banking experience preferred

json.companyDescription:
Banque Misr (BM) was established in 1920 by the pioneer economist and financial expert Mohamed Talaat Harb Pasha, who spearheaded the concept of investing in national savings and directing them toward economic and social development. Thus, Banque Misr was established as the first wholly Egyptian-owned bank.

Banque Misr has funded many businesses spanning across multiple domestic sectors, such as: textiles, insurance, transportation, aviation, entertainment, and filmmaking. Currently, BM owns shares in 157 companies across different fields, ranging from finance, tourism, housing, agriculture and food, and communication and information technology.

A true pioneer in the region, Banque Misr became the first bank in Egypt and North Africa to comply with PCI data security standards, upon obtaining the latest version of the global Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS 3.2.1) certification. Utilizing the latest technology in the banking sector, Banque Misr is constantly looking to expand customer access to banking services. Today, Banque Misr is proud to offer one of Egypt’s largest ATM networks, located across all areas of Egypt.

Banque Misr’s role is visible in all economic fields due to its geographic outreach. The bank has more than 20,000 employees, serving a large base of more than 13 million clients in Egypt, with a total paid-up capital amounting to EGP 15 billion.

The bank has more than 800 electronically integrated local branches located nationwide to provide the best and most accessible services to customers. Banque Misr also values its regional and international presence, which includes its five branches in the United Arab Emirates and one in France. In addition, the bank’s international presence includes subsidiaries in Lebanon and Germany, as well as representative offices in China, Russia, South Korea, and Italy and a global network of correspondents.

json.title: Clinical Data Specialist
json.descriptionHtml:
Key Responsibilities


  • Review, annotate, and label clinical and RCM-related datasets according to guidelines.
  • Apply clinical and insurance knowledge to interpret medical records and billing data.
  • Validate data quality, identify discrepancies, and escalate issues.
  • Collaborate with clinical, data science, QA, and operations teams.
  • Maintain clear documentation of annotation decisions.
  • Support reporting and basic analysis using Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Adapt to changing guidelines and project requirements.


Qualifications


  • Bachelor’s degree in pharmacy, Biotechnology, Medicine, or related field (required).
  • Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365 tools.
  • Familiarity with SQL and/or Python is a plus.
  • Experience working with structured and unstructured medical data.
  • 1-2 years of experience as a clinical pharmacist or in a clinical role.
  • 1-2 years of experience in medical insurance or Revenue Cycle Management (coding, claims, prior authorizations, denials, or utilization review) is a strong plus.
  • Prior exposure to clinical data annotation or auditing is advantageous.


Preferred Qualifications


  • Master’s degree in a clinical or healthcare field.
  • Masters's degree in Data Science or Data Labelling.


Soft Skills & Competencies


  • High attention to details with strong analytical and critical-thinking skills.
  • Clear written and verbal communication, with the ability to explain clinical decisions and findings.
  • Strong collaboration skills and comfort working in cross-functional teams.
  • Effective time management with the ability to meet deadlines in fast-paced, changing environments.
  • Flexible, adaptable, and open to continuous learning.

json.title: SQL Trainer
json.descriptionHtml:
Job Title: SQL Instructor for Data Analysis

 

 Role Overview:

As an SQL Instructor, you will be responsible for designing and delivering training sessions focused on SQL for Data Analysis.

You will guide learners in understanding data structures, writing queries, and applying SQL techniques to real-world business problems.

 

 Key Responsibilities:

  • Deliver interactive and engaging SQL sessions (Beginner to Advanced levels).
  • Explain SQL concepts clearly with practical business use cases.
  • Provide learners with hands-on exercises and projects using real datasets.
  • Collaborate with the operations and academic team to improve course content.


 Requirements:

  • Proven experience as an SQL Trainer / Instructor or in a similar teaching role.
  • Strong knowledge of SQL for Data Analysis (e.g., joins, subqueries, window functions, CTEs, data cleaning, and reporting).
  • Hands-on experience with MySQL / SQL Server / PostgreSQL.
  • Background in Data Analysis or BI tools (Power BI, Excel,) is a plus.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills.
  • Experience delivering online or offline training sessions.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.

json.title: Junior Data Analyst
json.descriptionHtml:
About Paymob
Paymob is a leading FinTech company revolutionizing payment solutions in the MENA region. At Paymob, we believe in enabling businesses to thrive by offering secure, innovative, and scalable tools that simplify payments for everyone.
We’re proud to power millions of transactions for some of the biggest names in the region, with a team of innovators spread across Egypt, UAE, KSA and Oman.
We foster a value driven culture focused on continuous learning, empowering our people, and collaborating as one global team to create real impact.
Job purpose:
We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented fresh graduate who is passionate about data and eager to build a career in analytics. The Junior Data Analyst will support business operations by extracting, cleaning, analyzing, and presenting data from multiple sources to enable informed decision-making and accurate reporting.

RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Extract reports and data from multiple systems, including Excel files, dashboards, and internal tools.
  • Clean, validate, and organize data to ensure accuracy and consistency.
  • Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports for different stakeholders.
  • Identify trends, issues, inconsistencies, and anomalies within datasets.
  • Develop simple dashboards using Excel and/or Power BI.
  • Use Python (Pandas) to automate repetitive reporting and data tasks when applicable.
  • Provide clear insights and summaries alongside reports, not only raw data.
  • Support continuous improvement of reporting processes and data workflows.


REQUIREMENTS
  • What We're Looking For:
  • Fresh graduate in a relevant field (Business, Economics, Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering, or related discipline).
  • Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel, including formulas, pivot tables, and reporting functions.
  • Basic knowledge of Python, especially Pandas library.
  • Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy.
  • Good analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
  • Basic English communication skills (written and spoken).
  • Basic SQL knowledge.
  • Familiarity with Power BI.
  • Experience with automation tools or scripting.


BENEFITS
  • We offer competitive compensation packages and performance driven incentive programs.
  • We offer a flexible hybrid work model that supports both in office collaboration and remote productivity.
  • We invest in continuous career coaching to support your growth and long-term development.
  • ·We collaborate across borders, embracing diversity and shared success.

json.companySlogan: On a mission to build the infrastructure that powers the digital economy
json.companyDescription:
Paymob is the leading financial services enabler in the MENA-P region. 

Our mission is to fuel SME growth by offering a payments infrastructure that delivers the most innovative digital payments solutions to businesses of all sizes. Our omnichannel financial technology platform enables merchants to accept online and offline payments, make payments, manage their finances and grow their businesses all in one place. We offer the most comprehensive suite of online and in store payments .

We power millions of transactions for some of the biggest names in our region like Foodics, IKEA, Uber and Shahid in addition to thousands of SMEs.

We serve the financial technology needs of over 390,000 merchants in the markets we operate and employ 1,100 team members with offices in Egypt, KSA, the UAE, Oman and Pakistan.

Founded in 2015, Paymob was the first fintech company to receive the Central Bank of Egypt’s (CBE) Payments Facilitator license in 2018. We launched operations in Pakistan in 2021 and in the UAE in 2022. Paymob received Saudi Payments PTSP certification in May 2023 enabling us to launch operations in KSA. In December 2023 Paymob became the first international fintech company to receive Oman’s PSP license.  In 2025 Paymob received the Central Bank of the UAE's Retail Payment Services license authorizing us to offer our full suite of solutions to merchants in the UAE market.

json.title: Software Engineer
json.descriptionHtml:
Company Description

CodeContent (CC Global Group, LLC) is a technology-driven organization dedicated to delivering cutting-edge software solutions that empower businesses. With a strong focus on innovation and efficiency, we develop tools and applications that cater to a wide range of industries. Our collaborative environment encourages professional growth, creativity, and high-quality software development. We are committed to maintaining a culture of excellence, diversity, and inclusion while contributing to technological advancements.

Role Description

We are seeking a skilled and motivated Software Engineer for a full-time on-site role located in Cairo. This position involves designing, developing, and maintaining software applications with a primary focus on back-end development and object-oriented programming. The Software Engineer will work closely with cross-functional teams to identify technical requirements, develop innovative solutions, and support coding, debugging, and software testing activities.

Qualifications
  • Strong understanding of Computer Science principles and practices
  • Proficiency in Back-End Web Development and Software Development
  • Experience in Programming with modern languages and frameworks
  • Expertise in Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
  • Problem-solving skills and ability to write efficient, scalable code
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field
  • Previous experience in similar roles is a plus

json.title: Full-Stack QA Engineer (Manual & Automation)
json.descriptionHtml:
Nexus Analytica, a leading provider of consultancy and digital solutions in the energy sector, is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Full-Stack QA Engineer (Manual & Automation) to join our growing team. This full-time position is based in our Cairo, Egypt office. The ideal candidate will be passionate about ensuring software quality and will play a crucial role in our development lifecycle, from refining requirements to final deployment. You will be responsible for testing across our entire technology stack, combining both manual and automated strategies to deliver robust and reliable solutions to our clients.

Salary: EGP 25,000 - EGP 40,000 per month

Responsibilities

  • Develop, document, and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases to ensure software quality.
  • Design, implement, and maintain automated test scripts for regression and performance testing.
  • Perform thorough manual testing for new features, bug fixes, and user interfaces.
  • Identify, record, and meticulously track defects through to resolution using bug tracking systems.
  • Conduct various testing types, including functional, integration, regression, and user acceptance testing.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, product managers, and other stakeholders to understand requirements and ensure a high-quality end product.
  • Participate actively in agile development processes, providing valuable input during planning and review stages.
  • Analyse test results and generate detailed reports on software quality and system performance.

Qualifications

  • Proven professional experience as a Quality Assurance Engineer with a strong background in both manual and automation testing.
  • Solid experience with automated testing frameworks and tools such as Selenium, Cypress, or Playwright.
  • Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language (e.g., Python, JavaScript, Java).
  • Experience with API testing tools like Postman or Insomnia.
  • A strong understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and agile methodologies.
  • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and version control systems like Git.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with an exceptional attention to detail.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
  • A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field is preferred but not essential.

Benefits

  • A competitive salary package and benefits.
  • Opportunities for continuous professional development and career progression.
  • A collaborative, supportive, and innovative work environment.
  • The opportunity to work on challenging and impactful projects within the dynamic energy sector.

json.title: Java Coding Specialist - Freelance AI Trainer Project
json.descriptionHtml:
Java Coding Specialist - Freelance AI Trainer Project United States of America

Are you a Java expert who can spot a flawed abstraction from across a codebase? Large-scale language models are evolving from clever chatbots into engines of real scientific and engineering progress. High-quality training data is what separates a capable model from a genuinely useful one. That data starts with people who actually know the craft.

We're looking for Java specialists with deep fluency in object-oriented design, concurrency, JVM internals, data structures, algorithms, and system architecture. You'll put advanced language models through their paces on Java problems spanning a wide range of difficulty and complexity, assessing whether model-generated solutions are correct, well-reasoned, and production-quality. That means evaluating not just whether code runs, but whether it's the right approach, whether edge cases are handled, and whether the reasoning behind it holds up.

You'll review Java problems and model-generated solutions, judge correctness and code quality, identify where the model's logic breaks down, and document failure patterns clearly enough that others can act on them. You may also flag whether a given problem is well-specified and appropriately challenging for training purposes.

A bachelor's, master's, or PhD in computer science, software engineering, or a closely related field is a strong signal. So is real-world Java experience, competitive programming, open-source contributions, or technical writing. The ability to clearly articulate why a solution is wrong or weak, not just that it is, matters as much as the technical depth.

Ready to put your Java expertise to work shaping the next generation of AI? Apply today.

We offer a pay of $45 per hour for candidates who successfully pass our preliminary Java-focused assessments. As a contractor, you'll supply your own secure computer and high-speed internet. Company-sponsored benefits such as health insurance and PTO do not apply.

Project Type: Freelance / Contract

Workplace type: Remote
